summary: Local-only HoMM3 terrain renderer prototype. Terrain and road frames are staged from extracted HoMM3 DEF assets for renderer study only, not for shippable/distributable art. Current logical terrain ids and save/pathing data remain unchanged, while the Godot map editor routes terrain paint operations through the recovered HoMM3 owner queue/rewrite and final-normalization placement model before shared preview reprojection. Active HoMM3 terrain visual selection now uses the accepted web prototype's settled-owner relation-class row lookup for normal terrain, water, and rock frames instead of the older source-offset stamp/corner heuristics. Rendering reports logical terrain id, renderer family, atlas role, relation ring, recovered shape class, row group, frame, transform flags, final-normalization metadata, and fallback metadata. Roads remain resolved through the existing 4-neighbor overlay masks. The editor base-terrain picker exposes only the curated HoMM3-style base family representatives declared in editor_base_terrain_options.

homm3_local_prototype.note: Local prototype only. These extracted original HoMM3 frames are not shippable or redistributable game assets.

subterranean_preferred_class_unresolved.note: Subterranean/subbtl has both dirt and sand receiver blocks; the preferred editor transition class is unresolved, so the current dirt-family fallback is explicitly provisional.

direct_bridge_material_contacts.note: A dirt/earth material neighbor touching a full receiver selects that receiver atlas' native-to-dirt transition block before frame lookup.
A sand material neighbor touching a full receiver selects that receiver atlas' native-to-sand transition block before frame lookup.
A dirt receiver beside direct sand uses dirttl's dirt-to-sand transition block, not sandtl as an edge-mask atlas.
Sand remains base/decor material; contact metadata is exposed for validation without treating sandtl as a generic edge-mask atlas.

preferred_bridge_class_routes.note: Water prefers the sand bridge class before watrtl shoreline lookup; sandtl does not own shoreline pixels.
Rock prefers the sand bridge class before rocktl-specific pale-ground/void lookup; it does not use normal land masks.
Grass, rough, snow, swamp, and lava prefer dirt/earth bridge class when no direct material or routed bridge rule is more specific.

unresolved_fallbacks.note: Subterranean/subbtl preferred bridge class remains unresolved; dirt is only the current data-driven provisional fallback.

direct_bridge_pairs.note: Dirt and swamp have a direct local HoMM3 transition path; do not route this pair through the sand bridge family.
Grass receivers near direct sand use grastl native-to-sand frames 00_20 through 00_39, not a separate extracted grass-sand DEF or JSON source and not the generic dirt bridge frames.

routed_bridge_rules.note: Grass/swamp contact is routed through dirt/earth bridge behavior rather than modeled as direct all-to-all native blending.

dirt.source_artifact_mapping: Full receiver land atlases expose frames 00_00 through 00_19 as native-to-dirt transition stamps.
dirt.source_offset_model: source_tile_minus_receiver_tile; exact original offsets are unrecovered and this table is a provisional array reconstruction fallback

sand.source_artifact_mapping: Full receiver land atlases expose frames 00_20 through 00_39 as native-to-sand transition stamps.
sand.source_offset_model: source_tile_minus_receiver_tile; exact original offsets are unrecovered and this table is a provisional array reconstruction fallback

accepted_web_prototype_parity.frame_selection: chooseFrame(rows, x, y, `${ownerId}:class:${shapeClass}`) from accepted web prototype
accepted_web_prototype_parity.note: Do not force 00_20 for sand-heavy corners here. The accepted web prototype classifies the same settled owner topology through relation class 12 and hashes within rows 36-37 with orientation flags.

dirt_receiver_transition.role: current direct-pair dirt receiver fallback until reduced receiver routing is tightened

rock_light_ground_context.role: rock/cliff against light or pale ground; exact topology unresolved

rock.provisional_fallback_policy: rocktl topology and passability semantics are unresolved; current lookup remains rock-system-specific and does not use generic land edge masks

base_context_provisional.role: current base-context fallback; sandtl is not a generic land edge-mask source

sand.provisional_fallback_policy: sandtl remains base/decor material; current neighbor-context selection is provisional and must not be treated as a recovered land edge-mask table

subterranean.provisional_fallback_policy: subbtl preferred bridge class is unresolved; current dirt-family fallback is data-driven and provisional

shoreline_frames.role: watrtl shoreline frames owned by water tiles

water.unresolved_fallback_policy: water-through-sand is a preferred bridge-class input before watrtl shoreline lookup; sandtl does not own shoreline pixels in current evidence

forest.logical_degrade_note: HoMM3 forest is primarily an object-layer concern; this local prototype maps the logical forest terrain id to the grass terrain atlas and keeps the limitation explicit.

underground.provisional_fallback_policy: subterranean preferred bridge class is unresolved
